reasoning: While Iran has significantly advanced its nuclear enrichment program and shortened its breakout time to a matter of days or weeks, US intelligence consistently reports that Iran has not yet made the political decision to weaponize. Weaponization itself—designing and testing a warhead—is estimated to take between several months to over a year. Even if Iran were to make the decision in 2024 or 2025 in response to geopolitical tensions, assembling and successfully testing a device before December 31, 2025, remains difficult, though technically possible. Furthermore, any move toward weaponization risks severe preemptive military strikes from Israel or the US, acting as a strong deterrent. The probability of Iran successfully building and possessing a confirmed nuclear weapon by the end of 2025 is low, though it is the highest it has been in years due to heightened regional conflicts.
